zh.lifeSummary: 當Jean comte de Hainaut et de Hollande I於1247年9月11日出生在法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯時，他的父親Jean Ier d'Avesnes29歲，母親Aleid van Holland gravin van Henegouwen21歲。他於大約1270年與Philippa de Luxembourg結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1304年8月22日在法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德瓦倫希恩斯享年56歲，埋葬在法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德瓦倫希恩斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jean comte de Hainaut et de Hollande I于1247年9月11日出生在法国諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯时，他的父亲Jean Ier d'Avesnes29岁，母亲Aleid van Holland gravin van Henegouwen21岁。他于大约1270年与Philippa de Luxembourg结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1304年8月22日在法国諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德瓦倫希恩斯享年56岁，埋葬在法国諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德瓦倫希恩斯。

nameMeanings.name: comte
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Jean Jacques.
French:

nickname for someone in the service of a count or for someone who behaved pretentiously, from Old French conte, cunte ‘count’. In North America, this surname is also a shortened form of the French cognate Lecomte . Compare Conte 3.

variants.name: Lecomte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Monique, Normand, Alain, Armand, Cecile, Dominique, Francois, Germain, Gilles, Gregoire, Huguette, Pierre.

French and Walloon: nickname for someone in the service of a count or for someone who behaved pretentiously, from Old French conte, cunte ‘count’, with fused masculine definite article le. Compare Comte 1.
History: This surname is listed (in the form Le Comte) in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Hollande
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of Holland , especially 3, with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Dutch: genitivized form of   Holland  .

variants.name: Holland
meanings: English, German, Dutch, Danish, Norwegian, Swedish, French, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from Holland, a province of the Netherlands.

English: habitational name from Downholland or Upholland (Lancashire), Hulland (Derbyshire), the Parts of Holland, one of the three administrative subdivisions of Lincolnshire, any of the four places called Hoyland (southern Yorkshire), and possibly Great and Little Holland (Essex). The placenames all derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + land ‘land’.

English: habitational name either from Hoeland (Farm) in Bury (Sussex), or from Holland's Barn in Albourne (Sussex). The placename in Bury has the same etymology as in 1 above, while the placename in Albourne may derive from Old English hol ‘hole, hollow’ + land ‘land’.

nameMeanings.name: Jean
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Like Jane and Joan , a medieval variant of Old French Je(h)anne. Towards the end of the Middle Ages this form became largely confined to Scotland. In the 20th century it became more widely used in the English-speaking world and enjoyed a period of great popularity, but it is now out of fashion. Among numerous well-known and influential bearers are the British novelists Jean Plaidy ( Eleanor Hibbert , 1910–93 ) and Jean Rhys ( Ella Gwendolen Rees Williams , 1894–1979 ), British actress Jean Simmons ( 1929–2010 ), and American-born actress Jean Seberg ( 1938–79 ). It is also found as a variant spelling of the masculine name Gene .

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

variants.name: Jeane
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jean .

en.lifeSummary: When Jean comte de Hainaut et de Hollande I was born on 11 September 1247, in Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, his father, Jean Ier d'Avesnes, was 29 and his mother, Aleid van Holland gravin van Henegouwen, was 21. He married Philippa de Luxembourg about 1270. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 22 August 1304, in Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, at the age of 56, and was buried in Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jean comte de Hainaut et de Hollande I est né le 11 septembre 1247, à Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, son père, Jean Ier d'Avesnes, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Aleid van Holland gravin van Henegouwen, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Philippa de Luxembourg environ 1270. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 22 août 1304, à Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, à l’âge de 56 ans, et a été enterré à Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France.

de.Frankfurt, Holy Roman Empire: Frankfurt am Main, Heiliges Römisches Reich
de.lifeSummary: Als Jean comte de Hainaut et de Hollande I am 11. September 1247 in Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Jean Ier d'Avesnes 29 und seine Mutter Aleid van Holland gravin van Henegouwen 21. Er hat um 1270 Philippa de Luxembourg geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb am 22. August 1304 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich und wurde in Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jean comte de Hainaut et de Hollande I è nato il il 11 settembre 1247 nel Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, il suo padre, Jean Ier d'Avesnes, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Aleid van Holland gravin van Henegouwen, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Philippa de Luxembourg circa 1270. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 22 agosto 1304, nel Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, all'età di 56 anni, e fu sepolto a Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Jean comte de Hainaut et de Hollande Iが1247年9月11日にフランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスで生まれた時，父Jean Ier d'Avesnesは29歳，母Aleid van Holland gravin van Henegouwenは21歳であった。約1270年にPhilippa de Luxembourgと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1304年8月22日にフランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスノードベレンシエンスで56歳で死去し，フランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスノードベレンシエンスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jean comte de Hainaut et de Hollande I(이)가 1247년 9월 11일 프랑스 노르드-파스-데-캘레이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Jean Ier d'Avesnes(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Aleid van Holland gravin van Henegouwen(은)는 21세였다. 그는 대략 1270년 Philippa de Luxembourg(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1304년 8월 22일 프랑스 노르드-파스-데-캘레이 노르드 발시네스에서 56세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 노르드-파스-데-캘레이 노르드 발시네스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jean comte de Hainaut et de Hollande I nasceu em 11 de setembro de 1247, em Nord-Pas-de-Calais, França, seu pai, Jean Ier d'Avesnes, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Aleid van Holland gravin van Henegouwen, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Philippa de Luxembourg aproximadamente 1270. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 22 de agosto de 1304, em Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, França, com 56 anos, e foi sepultado em Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jean comte de Hainaut et de Hollande I родился 11 сентября 1247 г. в местности норд-пас-де-калеи, Франция, его отцу по имени Jean Ier d'Avesnes было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Aleid van Holland gravin van Henegouwen, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Philippa de Luxembourg около 1270 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. Он умер 22 августа 1304 г. в местности веиленсиеннесь, норд, норд-пас-де-калеи, Франция в возрасте 56 лет и был похоронен в местности веиленсиеннесь, норд, норд-пас-де-калеи, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jean comte de Hainaut et de Hollande I nació el 11 de septiembre de 1247, en Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, su papá, Jean Ier d'Avesnes, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Aleid van Holland gravin van Henegouwen, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Philippa de Luxembourg aproximadamente 1270. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 22 de agosto de 1304, en Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, a la edad de 56 años, y fue sepultado en Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia.
